title: Universal optimality of the $E_8$ and Leech lattices and interpolation formulas

thm:1.4.summary: $E_8$ in $\mathbb{R}^8$ and the Leech lattice in $\mathbb{R}^{24}$ are *universally optimal*: among all periodic configurations they minimize every completely monotonic pairwise potential energy. Generalizes the sphere-packing optimality (Viazovska, CKMRV) to all such energies.

cor:1.5.summary: Among unit-determinant lattices $\Lambda$ in $d=8$ or $24$, the minimum value of $\sum_{v\in\Lambda\setminus 0}e^{-\pi|v|^2 t}$ is attained at $E_8$ (resp. Leech) for every $t>0$ — the lattice-theta-series specialization of universal optimality.

prop:1.6.summary: Schwartz upper-envelope lemma: for any $p:(0,\infty)\to[0,\infty)$ and Schwartz $f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}$ with $f(x)\le p(|x|)$, the corresponding sum over a periodic configuration is controlled by the envelope — the smoothing tool used to bridge potentials to LP duality.

thm:1.7.summary: Interpolation formula at $\sqrt{2n}$: for $(d,n_0)=(8,1)$ or $(24,2)$, every radial Schwartz $f$ on $\mathbb{R}^d$ is uniquely determined by the values $f(\sqrt{2n})$, $f'(\sqrt{2n})$, $\widehat{f}(\sqrt{2n})$, $\widehat{f}'(\sqrt{2n})$ for $n\ge n_0$ — the higher-dimensional analogue of Radchenko–Viazovska, with derivative data.

cor:1.8.summary: Uniqueness of the magic functions: in $\mathbb{R}^8$ and $\mathbb{R}^{24}$ the optimal auxiliary functions for the sphere-packing LP bound and for Gaussian-potential energy minimization are unique — the rigidity statement following from the interpolation formula.

thm:1.9.summary: Isomorphism form of interpolation: the value-and-derivative map sending $f\in\mathcal{S}_{\textup{rad}}(\mathbb{R}^d)$ to its samples at $\sqrt{2n}$ is an isomorphism onto an explicitly described space — the analogue of Radchenko–Viazovska's $\ker L$ characterization.

thm:3.1.summary: Functional-equation $\leftrightarrow$ interpolation framework: existence of smooth $F,\widetilde F:\mathbb{H}\times\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{C}$ holomorphic in $\tau$ and satisfying explicit modular functional equations is equivalent to an interpolation formula — the bridge from modular forms to sampling.

cor:3.5.summary: Group-algebra obstruction: $w = \sum_\gamma c_\gamma \gamma \notin (T-I)R$ if some $c_{(xy)^n\gamma}\ne 0$ for exactly one $n$ — distinguishes which group-algebra elements are coboundaries.

prop:3.8.summary: The sums defining the ideals $\mathcal{I}, \mathcal{I}_\pm$ of $R = \mathbb{C}[\mathrm{PSL}_2(\mathbb{Z})]$ are direct sums (free $R$-modules of rank 2) — the structural identification of the relevant ideals.

prop:3.9.summary: Dimensions and bases of the quotient algebras: $\dim_\mathbb{C} R/\mathcal{I} = 6$ with explicit basis $M_1=I, M_2=T, M_3=TS,\dots$ — the six-dimensional representation of $\mathrm{SL}_2(\mathbb{Z})$ that controls the interpolation.

prop:3.11.summary: Linear functionals $\phi_\pm: R/\mathcal{I}_\pm \to \mathbb{C}$ defined on the explicit basis — the duality between the algebra structure and the interpolation kernels.

prop:4.2.summary: Analytic-continuation lemma: continuous $h_1,h_2:\mathbb{H}\to\mathbb{C}$ satisfying matching slash-action conditions admit holomorphic extensions and obey the resulting functional equations — the analytic substrate for kernel existence.

thm:4.3.summary: Existence and uniqueness of the interpolation kernels $\mathcal{K}, \mathcal{K}_\pm$ for $d=8,24$: meromorphic functions encoding the interpolation basis, determined by their modular transformation properties and singularity locus.

prop:4.4.summary: Annihilator space $\mathrm{Ann}_k(\mathcal{I}_+,\mathcal{P})$ description: the solutions $f\in\mathcal{P}$ to $f|_k(T-I)^2 = 0$ and the analogous $\mathcal{I}_+$ relations form an explicit modular-form space at weight $k$ — the $+1$ side of the kernel.

prop:4.5.summary: Annihilator space $\mathrm{Ann}_k(\mathcal{I}_-,\mathcal{P})$: the $-1$ side of the kernel, also explicitly described in terms of modular forms.

prop:4.7.summary: Annihilator space $\mathrm{Ann}_k(\widetilde{\mathcal{I}}_+,\mathcal{P})$: solutions to $f|_k(T-2I+T^{-1}-2S) = 0$ — the second-derivative-data analogue, governing the $f'(\sqrt{2n})$ samples.

prop:4.8.summary: Annihilator space $\mathrm{Ann}_k(\widetilde{\mathcal{I}}_-,\mathcal{P})$: the $-1$ analogue of Proposition 4.7.

prop:4.10.summary: Kernel transformation property: $\mathcal{K}_\pm(\tau,z)|_{2-d/2}^z r = 0$ for the relevant slash actions — confirms the kernels live in the predicted annihilator spaces.

prop:5.1.summary: Analytic continuation of $\tau\mapsto F_2(\tau,r)$ from the fundamental domain $\mathcal{D}$ to an open neighborhood $\mathcal{D}_+\supset\overline{\mathcal{D}}$ — extends the interpolation generating function past the cusp.

cor:5.2.summary: $\tau\mapsto F_2(\tau,r)$ extends holomorphically to all of $\mathbb{H}$ and satisfies the explicit slash-action identities $F_2|^\tau_{d/2}(T-I)^2 = -e^{\pi i r^2\tau}|^\tau_{d/2}(T-I)^2$, etc.

prop:5.4.summary: Integral formulas for the interpolation basis elements — explicit contour-integral expressions used to evaluate and bound the basis functions in applications.

prop:6.1.summary: Kernel positivity reduction: the universal-optimality inequality reduces to showing the interpolation kernels $\mathcal{K}_\pm$ satisfy a positivity condition on a fundamental domain — the analytic statement that drives the proof of Theorem 1.4.

prop:6.3.summary: Refinement of the positivity statement near the diagonals, edges, and corners of the relevant region — the case decomposition computer-assisted positivity is verified by.

conjecture:7.2.summary: Open question: extension of universal optimality to other lattices or sphere configurations (e.g. higher-dimensional analogues, the $A_2$ hexagonal lattice in dim 2).

conjecture:7.5.summary: Open question: structural conjecture on the interpolation kernels in higher dimensions or for other special configurations.

edges.note: Universal optimality is read off the interpolation formula applied to test functions arising from completely monotonic potentials.
The optimality inequality reduces to positivity of the interpolation kernels (Proposition 6.1).
The case-by-case positivity refinement (diagonals/edges/corners) completes the proof of Theorem 1.4.
The Schwartz upper-envelope lemma bridges potentials to LP duality.
The lattice-theta-series statement is the Gaussian-potential case of universal optimality.
The interpolation formula is realized via the kernels $\mathcal{K},\mathcal{K}_\pm$ of Theorem 4.3.
Interpolation needs the holomorphic continuation of the generating function $F_2$.
Integral formulas for the interpolation basis are read off Proposition 5.4.
Uniqueness of the optimal auxiliary functions is the kernel statement of the interpolation isomorphism.
The isomorphism statement specifies the image of the interpolation map of Theorem 1.7.
The $+1$-kernel lives in the annihilator space of Proposition 4.4.
The $-1$-kernel lives in the annihilator space of Proposition 4.5.
Derivative-data kernels live in the second-order annihilator space (Proposition 4.7).
The second-order $-1$ annihilator space (Proposition 4.8) supplies the corresponding kernel.
Existence of the kernels uses the analytic continuation lemma (Proposition 4.2).
The kernels are characterized by the slash-action identity of Proposition 4.10.
Kernel existence uses the functional-equation $\leftrightarrow$ interpolation framework of Theorem 3.1.
The framework rests on the ideal-structure result for $R=\mathbb{C}[\mathrm{PSL}_2(\mathbb{Z})]$ (Proposition 3.8).
Uses the dimensions and explicit bases of $R/\mathcal{I}, R/\mathcal{I}_\pm$ (Proposition 3.9).
Uses the linear functionals $\phi_\pm$ (Proposition 3.11) realizing the duality.
The dimension and basis statements assume the direct-sum structure of the ideals.
The linear functionals are defined on the explicit basis of Proposition 3.9.
The framework uses the coboundary obstruction (Corollary 3.5).
The annihilator-space dimension matches the quotient-algebra description of Proposition 3.9.
Same algebraic input as Proposition 4.4.
Full-$\mathbb{H}$ holomorphic continuation follows from the local extension to $\mathcal{D}_+$.
The boundary-case positivity refines the global positivity statement.

main_results.description: Universal optimality of $E_8$ and Leech, the lattice-theta-series corollary, the higher-dimensional interpolation formula, the uniqueness of magic functions, and the isomorphism statement.

functional_eq_framework.description: The framework reducing interpolation to a functional-equation problem on $\mathbb{H}\times\mathbb{R}^d$.

group_algebra.description: The ideals of $R=\mathbb{C}[\mathrm{PSL}_2(\mathbb{Z})]$ and the six-dimensional quotient that controls the interpolation.

kernels.description: Existence and uniqueness of the interpolation kernels $\mathcal{K},\mathcal{K}_\pm$ for $d=8,24$, and the analytic-continuation lemma they rest on.

annihilator_spaces.description: The explicit modular-form annihilator spaces $\mathrm{Ann}_k(\mathcal{I}_\pm,\mathcal{P})$ and $\mathrm{Ann}_k(\widetilde{\mathcal{I}}_\pm,\mathcal{P})$ where the kernels live, plus the slash-action characterization.

continuation_and_integrals.description: Analytic continuation of the generating function $F_2$ from the fundamental domain to all of $\mathbb{H}$, the resulting slash identities, and integral formulas for the basis.

positivity.description: The reduction of universal optimality to kernel positivity and the boundary-case refinement.

open_questions.description: Open conjectures on extensions of universal optimality and kernel structure in other dimensions.

Viazovska-E8.title: The sphere packing problem in dimension 8 (Viazovska)

Leech.title: The sphere packing problem in dimension 24 (CKMRV)

CM.title: Some properties of optimal functions for sphere packing in dimensions 8 and 24 (Cohn–Miller)

RV.title: Fourier interpolation on the real line (Radchenko–Viazovska)

CK.title: Universally optimal distribution of points on spheres (Cohn–Kumar)
